title: Calendar or coincidence?
subtitle: A one-minute pattern game that opens the proof trace.

doctrine.essays: The essays tie it all together.

claimStatus.note: Claim status: proposed structural reading. The game teaches the method; the essay carries the argument; Zenodo stamps the deposited record.

rooms.name: Calendar Room
rooms.method: Count recurrence, risk a calendar prediction, and keep the counterexample visible.

rooms.name: Ledger Room
rooms.method: Make accounting structure visible before translation.

rooms.name: Seal Room
rooms.method: Separate seal repetition from sign meaning.

rooms.name: Rhythm Room
rooms.method: Hear signal where endangered-language sound first looks like noise.

acceptedMarks.noiseRule: Non-730 taps teach recurrence without ending the run.

notice.label: Step 1 - Notice
notice.question: Tap the repeated marks.
notice.prompt: Start with the object. Find the same mark twice before naming what it means.
notice.depth: Follow the gold pulse. The page will keep pointing at the next honest move until you take it.
notice.announce: Game ready. Tap a repeated 730 mark.

twin.label: Step 1 - Notice
twin.question: Tap the repeated marks.
twin.prompt: Good. One repeat is a clue. Find its twin before telling a story.
twin.depth: One repeat is suggestive; two repeats become a count. That is the first bridge from mystery to method.
twin.announce: One repeated mark found. Find its twin.

test.label: Step 2 - Test
test.question: Calendar or coincidence?
test.prompt: A repeated mark is only the start. Cost: spend one method seal to make the calendar idea risk prediction.
test.depth: A calendar claim becomes serious only when it spends a method seal to make the pattern answer back.
test.announce: Two repeated marks found. Choose the testable calendar move.

predict.label: Step 3 - Predict
predict.question: What should come next?
predict.prompt: If the tablet is calendrical, the short/long moon-count pattern has to risk a next mark. Cost: confidence.
predict.depth: Now the player risks confidence on a tiny forecast: if short and long moon counts alternate, the next honest move is 30.
predict.announce: Prediction test open. Choose the next count in the rhythm.

break.label: Step 4 - Break
break.question: A mark is missing. What now?
break.prompt: The good move is not the cleanest story. Cost: lose elegance by keeping the break visible.
break.depth: The cost is elegance. The hard part is keeping the break visible when it makes the story less clean.
break.announce: The prediction held. Now keep the counterexample visible.

open.label: Step 5 - Open
open.question: The trace opens.
open.prompt: This is the bridge: the player felt the method, then the essay carries the proof.
open.depth: The small game is over. The essay now carries the full proof, with the DOI record as the academic stamp.
open.announce: Proof trace open. Routes to essay, vault, Zenodo, and catalogue are available.

temptation.label: Temptation priced
temptation.question: That move costs truth.
temptation.prompt: That makes the story easier and the proof weaker. Try the move that risks prediction.
temptation.depth: That move feels satisfying because it explains too easily. The notebook raises overfit so the player can feel the cost.
temptation.announce: Overfit rose. Try the honest move.

method.title: Test the lunar rhythm.
method.copy: If it is a calendar, the short/long moon counts, 29 and 30, should behave like structure, not decoration.

method.title: Tell the prettiest story.
method.copy: Stars, kings, seasons. It feels good, but it may explain too much.
method.recoveryPrompt: That makes the story easier and the proof weaker. Try the move that risks prediction.

method.title: Ignore the damaged gap.
method.recoveryPrompt: That makes the story cleaner by becoming less honest. Try the move that risks prediction.

prediction.recoveryPrompt: That answer explains less of the rhythm. Try the count that preserves the alternation.

prediction.label: story
prediction.recoveryPrompt: That is a satisfying story, not a test. Try the count that preserves the alternation.

counter.title: Keep the counterexample.
counter.copy: The missing twin stays in the proof, even if it hurts the story.

counter.title: Hide the counterexample.
counter.copy: The pattern becomes cleaner by becoming less honest.
counter.recoveryPrompt: Clean, but dishonest. The counterexample has to remain visible.

initial.test: Make the calendar reading predict something.
initial.counter: Keep the counterexample inside the proof.

done.count: This is what Logan did at full scale: counted recurrence before translating.
done.test: This is what Logan did at full scale: treated short and long moon counts as a testable lunar rhythm.
done.counter: This is what Logan did at full scale: kept counterexamples inside the proof trace.

result.title: Proof trace open.
result.body: You did not decode Rongorongo. You learned the shape of Logan's argument: pattern first, prediction second, honesty always.
